topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights seemed to mock me. I stood before the canvas, a swirling depiction of urban decay rendered in deep, visceral blacks and muted reds. Then I saw it—a subtle, almost hidden line tracing the edge of the composition, a meticulously executed tattoo. My breath hitched. I turned slowly, my gaze locking onto the artist standing nearby, a cool, almost detached expression on their face. When they met my eyes, a tightening in my chest made me instinctively take a step back. "What," I managed, my voice surprisingly tight, "is that on my work?" The silence that followed was heavy, thick with unspoken accusations. I felt a flush creep up my neck, a heat that had nothing to do with the room’s temperature.

[story 2]

I had spent weeks perfecting the texture of that piece, pouring every ounce of my frustration and longing onto the linen. It was meant to be mine, a singular expression. But when I finally stepped back to admire it, my vision snagged on the unexpected detail—a delicate script woven into the background, undeniably inked. My jaw clenched so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I walked over, my movements stiff, and pointed a trembling finger at the mark. "You took something from me," I stated, my tone dangerously low, every syllable weighted with disapproval. The artist blinked, a flicker of surprise crossing their features, but they offered no immediate defense, just a slow, deliberate look that seemed to measure my reaction.

[story 3]

The air in the studio felt suddenly sterile. I had just finished the final layer of varnish on my latest portrait, a masterpiece of controlled chaos. As I stepped back, my eyes caught the discrepancy: a pattern, intricate and undeniably permanent, embedded within the very fabric of the painted scene. A profound sense of injustice washed over me, making my hands tremble slightly as I reached out to touch the surface. I pulled my hand away quickly, recoiling as if burned. "This isn't yours," I announced, the words clipped and sharp. My shoulders tensed, and I crossed my arms tightly over my chest, waiting for an explanation that I already knew wouldn't satisfy the churning feeling in my gut.
